prescribing_info.title: KEPPRA (levetiracetam) Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
KEPPRA (LEVETIRACETAM) - FULL PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: UCB

INDICATIONS:
- Partial onset seizures (monotherapy and adjunctive)
- Myoclonic seizures in juvenile myoclonic epilepsy (adjunctive)
- Primary generalized tonic-clonic seizures (adjunctive)

DOSAGE - ADULTS:

Partial Onset Seizures:
- Initial: 500 mg twice daily
- Titration: Increase by 500 mg twice daily every 2 weeks
- Target: 1500 mg twice daily (3000 mg/day)
- MAXIMUM: 3000 mg/day (1500 mg twice daily)

Myoclonic Seizures:
- Initial: 500 mg twice daily
- Titration: Increase by 500 mg twice daily every 2 weeks
- MAXIMUM: 3000 mg/day

Generalized Tonic-Clonic:
- Initial: 500 mg twice daily
- Titration: Increase by 500 mg twice daily every 2 weeks
- MAXIMUM: 3000 mg/day

PEDIATRIC DOSING:

1 month to <6 months:
- Initial: 7 mg/kg twice daily
- Target: 21 mg/kg twice daily
- Maximum: 42 mg/kg/day

6 months to <4 years:
- Initial: 10 mg/kg twice daily
- Increase every 2 weeks by 10 mg/kg twice daily
- Maximum: 50 mg/kg/day (25 mg/kg twice daily)

4 to <16 years:
- Initial: 10 mg/kg twice daily
- Target: 30 mg/kg twice daily
- Maximum: 60 mg/kg/day (not to exceed 3000 mg/day)

RENAL DOSING (Adults):
- CrCl >80: 500-1500 mg twice daily
- CrCl 50-80: 500-1000 mg twice daily
- CrCl 30-50: 250-750 mg twice daily
- CrCl <30: 250-500 mg twice daily
- ESRD on dialysis: 500-1000 mg once daily + 250-500 mg supplemental after dialysis

ADMINISTRATION:
- May take with or without food
- Tablets: Swallow whole or may be broken
- Oral solution: Use calibrated measuring device
- IV: Dilute in 100 mL NS, LR, or D5W; infuse over 15 minutes

WARNINGS:
- Behavioral abnormalities: Aggression, irritability, mood changes
- Somnolence and fatigue
- Coordination difficulties
- Serious dermatologic reactions (SJS/TEN)
- Suicidal behavior (class warning for AEDs)

ADVERSE REACTIONS:
- Somnolence (15%)
- Asthenia (15%)
- Infection (13%)
- Dizziness (9%)
- Behavioral: Irritability, mood swings, aggression

DISCONTINUATION:
- Taper gradually
- Reduce dose by 500 mg twice daily every 2 weeks
- Abrupt withdrawal may increase seizure frequency

PREGNANCY:
- Pregnancy registry available
- Weigh benefits vs risks
- Potential increased clearance in pregnancy

HOW SUPPLIED:
- 250 mg, 500 mg, 750 mg, 1000 mg tablets
- 250 mg, 500 mg, 750 mg, 1000 mg XR tablets
- 100 mg/mL oral solution
- 500 mg/5 mL IV solution

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: NDA 021035
Label Revision: 08/2023
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=96dc0165-68e0-48eb-a6d7-3c42f51c72c9

prescribing_info.title: LAMICTAL (lamotrigine) Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
LAMICTAL (LAMOTRIGINE) - FULL PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: GlaxoSmithKline

BLACK BOX WARNING:
SERIOUS SKIN RASHES - Lamotrigine can cause serious, life-threatening skin rashes including Stevens-Johnson syndrome (SJS) and toxic epidermal necrolysis (TEN). Risk is higher with:
- Higher than recommended initial doses
- Exceeding recommended dose escalation
- Concomitant valproate
- Pediatric patients

INDICATIONS:
- Epilepsy (partial seizures, generalized seizures, Lennox-Gastaut syndrome)
- Bipolar I disorder (maintenance to delay mood episodes)

CRITICAL DOSING PRINCIPLE:
Dosing depends on concomitant medications. MUST follow specific escalation schedules to minimize rash risk.

ADULT EPILEPSY - WITHOUT VALPROATE, NOT ON ENZYME INDUCERS:
- Weeks 1-2: 25 mg once daily
- Weeks 3-4: 50 mg once daily
- Week 5: 100 mg once daily
- Week 6: 150 mg once daily
- Week 7+: 150-250 mg once daily (or divided)
- MAXIMUM: 400-600 mg/day (monotherapy)

ADULT EPILEPSY - WITH VALPROATE:
- Weeks 1-2: 25 mg every other day
- Weeks 3-4: 25 mg once daily
- Week 5: 50 mg once daily
- Week 6+: Increase by 25-50 mg every 1-2 weeks
- MAXIMUM: 200 mg/day (with valproate)

ADULT EPILEPSY - WITH ENZYME INDUCERS (carbamazepine, phenytoin, phenobarbital):
- Weeks 1-2: 50 mg once daily
- Weeks 3-4: 100 mg once daily (divided doses)
- Week 5+: Increase by 100 mg every 1-2 weeks
- MAXIMUM: 400-700 mg/day (divided twice daily)

BIPOLAR DISORDER (Not on enzyme-inducing drugs or valproate):
- Weeks 1-2: 25 mg once daily
- Weeks 3-4: 50 mg once daily
- Week 5: 100 mg once daily
- Week 6: 200 mg once daily
- Target: 200 mg/day
- MAXIMUM: 400 mg/day

PEDIATRIC DOSING (2-12 years):
- Follow mg/kg dosing with same valproate/enzyme inducer considerations
- Week 1-2 (with valproate): 0.15 mg/kg/day
- Week 1-2 (without valproate): 0.3 mg/kg/day
- Maximum varies by concomitant drugs

RENAL IMPAIRMENT:
- Mild-moderate: Usually no adjustment
- Severe/ESRD: Reduce maintenance by ~50%
- Hemodialysis: Give supplemental dose after dialysis

HEPATIC IMPAIRMENT:
- Moderate (Child-Pugh B): Reduce by 25%
- Severe (Child-Pugh C): Reduce by 50%

RASH PRECAUTIONS:
- STOP immediately if rash develops
- Risk factors: Fast titration, concomitant valproate, pediatric age
- Rash most common in first 8 weeks
- SJS/TEN incidence: 0.3-0.8% (pediatric), 0.08-0.3% (adult)

WARNINGS:
- Serious rash (SJS/TEN)
- Hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH)
- Multi-organ hypersensitivity (DRESS)
- Blood dyscrasias
- Suicidal behavior (class warning)
- Aseptic meningitis

DRUG INTERACTIONS:
- Valproate: Inhibits lamotrigine metabolism (reduce dose)
- Carbamazepine, phenytoin, phenobarbital: Induce metabolism (increase dose)
- Oral contraceptives: May decrease lamotrigine levels; lamotrigine may decrease contraceptive efficacy

HOW SUPPLIED:
- 25, 100, 150, 200 mg tablets
- 2, 5, 25 mg chewable dispersible tablets
- 25, 50, 100, 200 mg orally disintegrating tablets
- XR tablets: 25, 50, 100, 200, 250, 300 mg

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: NDA 020241
Label Revision: 10/2023
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=1a6d75f9-8e02-44b8-8235-e7b5c7c2b8d6

prescribing_info.title: AIMOVIG (erenumab) Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
AIMOVIG (ERENUMAB-AOOE) - FULL PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: Amgen/Novartis

MECHANISM: Human monoclonal antibody that binds to the calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP) receptor

INDICATION:
Preventive treatment of migraine in adults

DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION:
- Recommended dose: 70 mg subcutaneously once monthly
- Alternative: 140 mg subcutaneously once monthly
- 140 mg dose: Administer as two consecutive 70 mg injections

DOSE SELECTION:
- Start with 70 mg monthly
- Some patients may benefit from 140 mg monthly
- If inadequate response at 70 mg after 3 months, consider 140 mg

MAXIMUM DOSE:
- 140 mg per month
- Do not exceed 140 mg in any month

ADMINISTRATION:
- Subcutaneous injection only
- Injection sites: Abdomen, thigh, or upper arm
- Rotate injection sites
- Allow to reach room temperature before injection
- Administer within 7 days of removal from refrigerator
- Use entire contents of autoinjector or syringe

AUTOINJECTOR INSTRUCTIONS:
- Remove from refrigerator 30 minutes before use
- Inject over 15 seconds (70 mg) or 30 seconds (140 mg)
- Do not shake
- Protect from light

CONTRAINDICATIONS:
- Serious hypersensitivity to erenumab-aooe or any excipients

WARNINGS AND PRECAUTIONS:

Hypersensitivity Reactions:
- Anaphylaxis, angioedema, urticaria, dyspnea reported
- May occur days after administration
- Discontinue if serious hypersensitivity occurs

Constipation:
- Serious constipation reported with severe complications
- Monitor patients, especially those with pre-existing constipation
- Consider laxatives/stool softeners
- Some cases required hospitalization

Hypertension:
- New or worsening hypertension reported
- Some required intervention
- Monitor blood pressure during treatment

ADVERSE REACTIONS:
- Injection site reactions (6%)
- Constipation (3%)
- Muscle spasms/cramps (2%)
- Hypersensitivity (<1%)

USE IN SPECIFIC POPULATIONS:

Pregnancy:
- Limited data
- Pregnancy exposure registry available
- Consider developmental period (antibody transport occurs in third trimester)

Lactation:
- Unknown if present in breast milk
- Consider benefits vs risks

Pediatric:
- Safety/efficacy not established in patients <18 years

Renal/Hepatic Impairment:
- No formal studies
- Not expected to affect pharmacokinetics (monoclonal antibody)

Geriatric:
- Limited data in patients ≥65 years
- No dose adjustment expected

IMMUNOGENICITY:
- Anti-erenumab antibodies detected in some patients
- Neutralizing antibodies rare
- Clinical significance unclear

STORAGE:
- Refrigerate 2-8°C (36-46°F)
- Protect from light
- May store at room temperature up to 25°C (77°F) for up to 7 days
- Discard if left at room temperature >7 days
- Do not freeze
- Do not shake

HOW SUPPLIED:
- 70 mg/mL in single-dose prefilled autoinjector
- 70 mg/mL in single-dose prefilled syringe
- 140 mg/mL in single-dose prefilled autoinjector
- 140 mg/mL in single-dose prefilled syringe

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: BLA 761077
Label Revision: 06/2023
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=9a8b7c6d-5e4f-3g2h-1i0j-k9l8m7n6o5p4
